# Daxchain

> Daxchain is a small blockchain lab building practical Ethereum tooling and experimental tokens. Every project is open source on GitHub under the daxchain-io organization. The website at https://daxchain.io is a single static page that links to the project repositories.

This file is the full-content companion to https://daxchain.io/llms.txt — it inlines
the key descriptions so a language model can ingest everything in one request without
following links. Source of truth for each project is its GitHub repository.

- Website: https://daxchain.io
- GitHub organization: https://github.com/daxchain-io
- Website source: https://github.com/daxchain-io/daxchain-io.github.io

---

## EVM Tools

Repository: https://github.com/daxchain-io/evm-tools
Language: Go

A suite of composable command-line tools for observing EVM chains and moving that data
into downstream systems. Each tool does one job and speaks a single common data
contract — newline-delimited JSON (JSONL) on standard output — so they pipe together
cleanly. stdout carries the data stream; stderr carries human-readable diagnostics, so
the two never mix.

The suite is nine CLIs that share one configuration namespace — two producers and a
growing set of sinks:

- `evm-stream` — live EVM activity monitoring (contract events and native ETH transfers) emitted as JSONL.
- `evm-balance` — balance and contract-state polling emitted as JSONL.
- `evm-sink-kafka` — publish each record to Kafka topics, at-least-once.
- `evm-sink-webhook` — forward each record over HTTP, at-least-once, with optional filters.
- `evm-sink-file` — append each record to a rotating local file, at-least-once, with optional gzip compression and filters.
- `evm-sink-postgres` — insert each record into a PostgreSQL table; idempotent (`ON CONFLICT (dedup_key) DO NOTHING`), so at-least-once is effectively exactly-once in the table.
- `evm-sink-redis` — append each record to a Redis Stream (`XADD`), at-least-once; idempotent by default (a dedup-gated append keyed on `dedup_key`), with optional `MAXLEN` trimming.
- `evm-sink-aws-sqs` — send each record to an AWS SQS queue, at-least-once, FIFO-aware (credentials from the AWS default chain).
- `evm-sink-aws-sns` — publish each record to an AWS SNS topic, at-least-once, FIFO-aware.

Producers (`evm-stream`, `evm-balance`) emit records; any of the sinks above consume
them. The shape is always the same: a
producer writes JSONL to stdout and a sink reads it from stdin, for example
`evm-stream run -c chain.toml | evm-sink-kafka run -c chain.toml`. Installation is one
command for the whole suite via a Homebrew cask (`brew install --cask
daxchain-io/tap/evm-tools`) or a cosign-verified install script. A multi-stage
Dockerfile builds an Alpine image containing all nine binaries.

## Daxie

Repository: https://github.com/daxchain-io/daxie
Language: Go

The Ethereum wallet for AI — an AI-agent-first Ethereum CLI wallet with a built-in MCP
(Model Context Protocol) server. Most Ethereum tooling assumes a human at a terminal
confirming each action; Daxie inverts that, building for an autonomous AI agent to hold an
account and move funds within operator-set limits it cannot raise.

Design highlights: non-interactive flags/env/stdin, `--json` everywhere, deterministic
exit codes, sealed spend-limit guardrails, and a one-core/two-frontends architecture so
the CLI and the MCP server traverse the exact same wallet logic and the exact same
guardrails. Custody in v1 is an encrypted local keystore inside one OS trust domain
(the same uid as the AI agent); the project documents that boundary honestly and describes
a v2 signer-daemon path.

## NZT-48

Repository: https://github.com/daxchain-io/nzt48
Language: Solidity

An experimental ERC-20 for playing with a strange idea: everybody already has some.
Most ERC-20s start every address at zero and wait for a transfer in. NZT-48 does the
opposite — if an ordinary address has no materialized balance, `balanceOf` reports a
default balance anyway (the default starts at 5,000 NZT-48). That makes it a toy coin
and a little on-chain oddity, built to poke at the assumptions wallets, indexers,
contracts, and people make about balances.

It is deployed on Ethereum Mainnet and Sepolia at
`0x30a580e9fcbdec9784122963cc3762afba57f10d` (these are the canonical deployments; no
additional canonical deployments are planned). Deployment receipts, ABI, and explorer
links live in the repository's `deployments/` directory.

## Blockchain Exporter

Repository: https://github.com/daxchain-io/blockchain-exporter
Language: Python
Status: Deprecated (maintenance only)

A Prometheus exporter for blockchain monitoring — a FastAPI service (Python 3.11) that
polls Ethereum-compatible chains and exposes metrics in Prometheus format, with health
endpoints for Kubernetes probes and a Helm chart and Docker image for deployment.

This project is deprecated and in maintenance mode. EVM-native tooling has matured to
cover its job more directly, so active development has moved to EVM Tools
(https://github.com/daxchain-io/evm-tools). The repository stays available as a
reference and for existing deployments; maintenance is limited to security and
dependency updates — now handled by light, AI-assisted upkeep — and no new features are
planned. For new work, start with EVM Tools.
